note: LIVING, append-only catalogue of the shapes a conversion fix can take. Seeded from every decision point + structural slot in the pipeline. A post-mortem that finds a NEW shape appends an entry here (status 'discovered', added_in the run date). 'scope' = who acts: model (DeepSeek, rendered into its prompt as an actionable fix-menu) | harness (us, improving the report/prompt/format) | disposition (a verdict, not a fix). 'expressible' = whether the current patch format can apply it: replace (full-function swap) | additive (needs op:add / op:register) | na.

categories.name: Tune a threshold / gate
categories.symptom: A decision sat near a numeric gate (margin shows 'N vs gate'); a slightly different cutoff would flip it. Low-confidence strategy/linkability/citation forks.
categories.slot: a numeric constant inside an existing function (position_ratio 0.65/0.80, fuzzy-year ±3, orphan-share 0.15)
categories.recipe: op:replace the function holding the constant; change ONLY the threshold; keep the modus operandi (no confident wrong links).
categories.risk: low blast radius, but a threshold tune can regress other documents — re-run the corpus after.

categories.name: Add an EPUB footnote/structure detector
categories.symptom: An EPUB's footnotes/markers weren't detected; epub_footnote_detection fired nothing, footnotes_matched is 0 but the source clearly has notes in a shape no detector recognises.
categories.slot: a NEW EpubTransform subclass in footnoteMatching.py + register it in the TRANSFORM_PIPELINE list (which lives in app/Python/ingestion/epub/epub_normalizer.py)
categories.recipe: op:add a class subclassing EpubTransform (from ingestion.epub.epub_base) in footnoteMatching.py with detect(soup)->bool (inspect only) and transform(soup, log_fn)->None (mutate when detect fired); then op:register the instance into TRANSFORM_PIPELINE (in epub_normalizer.py) in the footnote-detection phase.
categories.risk: low — detectors are composable and independent; a new one only fires when its detect() matches.

categories.name: Register a declarative EPUB footnote scheme (no class to write)
categories.symptom: An EPUB's footnotes are ID-ANCHORED — an in-text (often with a ) points at a definition by id (an empty or an Note N), with the note text in the following block. The raw-marker samples show the scheme but footnotes_matched is 0. PREFER THIS over hand-writing a detector — it can't crash at runtime.
categories.slot: the TRANSFORM_PIPELINE list in epub_normalizer.py (the registration point) — AnchoredFootnoteScheme is already defined+imported, so NO op:add is needed.
categories.recipe: op:register ONE expression into TRANSFORM_PIPELINE (in epub_normalizer.py): AnchoredFootnoteScheme(name='MySchemeFootnoteDetector', marker='sup-link'|'any-href', definition='empty-anchor'|'note-heading', content='following-siblings', boundary='heading-or-anchor'|'heading', strip_number=true|false). Pick marker/definition from the RAW-marker samples in the prompt: sup-link = the carries a ; empty-anchor = the definition is an empty whose note text follows. Pairs by id (never number); a target with no following note block is excluded, so it won't over-match TOC links.
categories.risk: very low — declarative parameters validated by the factory; if the scheme doesn't match it yields 0 footnotes (gate rejects), and a runtime error is caught per-detector. No bespoke logic to get wrong.

categories.name: Extend a marker / citation pattern
categories.symptom: Markers exist but in a shape the current regex/detector misses ([Author YEAR] square brackets, daggers/asterisks, lettered notes, superscript-in-link).
categories.slot: the recognition regex / branch inside an existing detector or scan function
categories.recipe: op:replace the function to broaden its pattern; or op:add a sibling scan and call it. Never widen a pattern so far it produces confident-wrong links.
categories.risk: medium — a looser pattern can mis-fire on non-markers; lean on compare_footnote_links.

categories.name: Add a new footnote strategy / fork
categories.symptom: The document's layout matches none of the existing strategies; strategy_selection fell through to a low-confidence default (e.g. footnotes AND citations both massed at the end; per-segment continuous numbering with an offset).
categories.slot: a new StrategyRule registered into STRATEGY_RULES (the ordered decision registry) + a new value in _ALL_STRATEGIES + a case in _strategy_considered
categories.recipe: op:add a class subclassing StrategyRule with matches(self, sig)->bool (reads the unrounded structure signals: has_footnote_resets, has_distributed_hrs, hr_count, footnotes_at_end, position_ratio, ref_position_ratio, …), a `strategy`/`reason`, and confidence(sig)/margin(sig); op:register it into STRATEGY_RULES at the right priority (first match wins; DefaultStrategyRule is the catch-all fallback, NOT in the list). Also op:register the name into _ALL_STRATEGIES and op:replace _strategy_considered to explain why the others lose. Then the linker must handle the new strategy.
categories.risk: high — a new strategy reshapes linking; verify against compare_footnote_links and that existing fixtures stay green. Adding a rule is safe; the signal computation in analyze_document_structure may also need a new signal.

categories.name: Add a new pipeline pass / stage
categories.symptom: No existing stage owns the problem — it needs a whole new phase (a cross-segment footnote reconciliation; an offset-correction pass; a new pre-processing step before extraction).
categories.slot: a NEW DocPass subclass + register it into the DOC_PASSES list (the ordered orchestration registry)
categories.recipe: op:add a class subclassing DocPass (from shared.pipeline_base) with apply(self, ctx) that reads/writes the shared DocContext (soup + the accumulators) and is GUARDED where it should only run on one branch (e.g. `if ctx.is_stem: return`); then op:register the instance into DOC_PASSES at the correct ORDER (extract precedes link precedes audit; node-gen + sanitize stay last). Record an ASSESSMENT fork for any new decision. A new step is absorbed by adding a pass, never by editing main().
categories.risk: medium — passes are small, ordered, guarded + independently unit-tested; the golden regression catches drift. Don't reorder existing passes (the id-generation sequence must hold).

categories.name: Fix ref-key generation / matching
categories.symptom: Citations found but unlinked; evidence.unlinked_sample shows keys that should have matched (et al., multi-author, transliteration, year-suffix collisions, unicode names).
categories.slot: generate_ref_keys / the key-match logic
categories.recipe: op:replace the key generator or matcher to cover the missed shape. Keep the ±-year fuzzy bound conservative — never match across distinct works.
categories.risk: medium — looser matching risks wrong-work links; verify the unlinked_sample now matches its TRUE entry.

categories.name: Fix the extract→apply linking seam
categories.symptom: Markers and definitions both exist and correspond, but linking cross-wires / off-by-ones / leaks across section scope (audit shows unmatched on both sides despite matching numbers).
categories.slot: the marker→definition map build or the anchor-apply step
categories.recipe: op:replace the extract or apply half to correct the pairing. A marker labelled N must carry the id of definition N (per test_linking).
categories.risk: medium — central to correctness; covered by unit/test_linking.py + compare_footnote_links.

categories.name: Add a footnote/citation LinkRule
categories.symptom: Markers and definitions correspond but link in a shape no existing rule handles — a new noteref/marker scheme, a new in-text citation delimiter, a dedup/cleanup the current rules miss. The right fix is a NEW ordered step in a linking registry, not surgery on a scan.
categories.slot: a NEW LinkRule subclass + register it into FOOTNOTE_LINK_RULES / MARKER_LINK_RULES / CITATION_LINK_RULES
categories.recipe: op:add a class subclassing LinkRule with apply(self, ctx, log) that reads/writes the shared Context (id_mapping, the accumulators) and mutates the soup; then op:register the instance into the right *_LINK_RULES list at the correct ORDER (id-mapping build precedes the conversion loop; the gate precedes the scans). A new file variant is absorbed by adding a rule, never by editing one.
categories.risk: low — rules are small, ordered, independently unit-tested; the golden regression + compare_orphans gate catches drift. Don't reorder existing rules.

categories.name: Fix segmentation / boundary detection
categories.symptom: Chapter/segment boundaries mis-detected so per-section numbering merges or splits wrongly — e.g. all definitions pooled while references cover only one segment's range (the aarushi 239–476 / 477 case).
categories.slot: the segment-boundary detection, or a reconciliation pass that scopes numbering per segment
categories.recipe: op:replace the boundary detector, or op:add a reconciliation pass that matches refs to defs within the correct segment/offset rather than globally.
categories.risk: high — structural; easy to make it worse. Often pairs with add_pipeline_pass.

categories.name: Fix structural normalization (pre-detection)
categories.symptom: Malformed upstream HTML blocks detection (calibre spans, nested divs, blockquote-wrapping, dead internal links) so downstream detectors never see clean structure.
categories.slot: a structural EpubTransform (phase 1) in structuralNormalisation.py — extend an existing one or add a new one (register in TRANSFORM_PIPELINE, which lives in epub_normalizer.py)
categories.recipe: op:replace an existing structural transform in structuralNormalisation.py, or op:add a new EpubTransform there + op:register it earlier in TRANSFORM_PIPELINE (in epub_normalizer.py) so footnote detectors then fire.
categories.risk: medium — runs on every EPUB; keep detect() narrow.

categories.name: Fix PDF OCR assembly (post-cache)
categories.symptom: PDF footnotes mis-assembled: page-bottom vs end-of-doc, two-column bleed, running headers leaking, page-footnote renumbering off; OR a new PDF/OCR-JSON shape the classifier mis-labels (there are endlessly many).
categories.slot: the PDF_CLASSIFIERS registry (classification.py — a PdfClassifier per layout) and the PDF_ASSEMBLERS registry (assembly.py — a FootnoteAssembler per layout — setup/per_page/post_combine), plus the shared helpers (pdf_shared.py: renumber_page_footnotes etc.). All replayed from cached OCR.
categories.recipe: Prefer ADDITIVE: for a NEW PDF layout, op:add a PdfClassifier (its matches/confidence/story) + op:register into PDF_CLASSIFIERS at the right priority, AND op:add a FootnoteAssembler + op:edit PDF_ASSEMBLERS to map the classification → assembler. For an existing layout, op:replace the relevant classifier/assembler method or shared helper. Do NOT touch fetch_ocr/extract_footer/extract_header — those only affect a fresh OCR and cannot be validated against this cached document.
categories.risk: medium — classifiers/assemblers are small, ordered, independently testable; validated by replaying cached OCR through assembly→html→process_document + the markdown snapshot.

categories.name: Fix the audit / verdict logic (a SIGNAL fix)
categories.symptom: The audit mis-measures — declares 'clean' despite real faults, or miscounts gaps/duplicates/unmatched. Fixing this improves the SIGNAL the loop and humans rely on, not the conversion itself.
categories.slot: compute_footnote_audit or the verdict/decision derivation
categories.recipe: op:replace the audit function so the verdict reflects all fault classes (a large unmatched-def share is faulty, not clean).
categories.risk: low for conversion output; it changes the assessment goldens, so update them.

categories.name: Strengthen a suppression guard (the honest non-link)
categories.symptom: Links were emitted that might be wrong (numbering can't be trusted) — the right fix is to NOT link, turning a confident-wrong link into an honest missing one.
categories.slot: _footnote_numbering_is_linkable (the linkability guard)
categories.recipe: op:replace the guard to suppress when the numbering correspondence isn't provably safe. A missing link is honest; a confident wrong link is not.
categories.risk: low — strictly safer; may reduce link counts (acceptable per modus operandi).

categories.name: Fix sanitization / encoding
categories.symptom: Mojibake / font-encoding / entity corruption garbles markers or note text so they can't be matched (font_encoding_warning_count > 0).
categories.slot: the sanitize/normalize functions
categories.recipe: op:replace the sanitizer to repair the specific encoding artefact before detection/linking.
categories.risk: medium — over-aggressive normalization can corrupt good text; scope the repair narrowly.

categories.name: Enrich the assessment signal (harness)
categories.symptom: The pipeline didn't RECORD enough to diagnose the case — a silent branch, missing evidence, or a fork with no 'considered'/would_need. The real defect never surfaced in the flagged forks.
categories.file: the responsible conversion module (add ASSESSMENT.record) + flagger in vibe_convert._is_problem
categories.slot: a new ASSESSMENT.record(...) call, or richer evidence on an existing one
categories.recipe: WE add the record/evidence so the next run surfaces the defect. Not something DeepSeek can do — it's a report fix.
categories.risk: low — diagnostic only, never alters conversion output.

categories.name: Improve the prompt / fix-taxonomy (harness)
categories.symptom: The signal was present and the fix expressible, but the prompt lacked the vocabulary/permission/recipe for DeepSeek to act — or this very registry was missing/weak on the needed category.
categories.slot: the prompt assembly, or a category entry's recipe/symptom
categories.recipe: WE sharpen the prompt or the category recipe, then re-run the corpus to measure if DeepSeek's success rate rises.
categories.risk: this is the core co-evolution lever; measured by Δscoreboard.

categories.name: Extend the patch-apply format (harness)
categories.symptom: DeepSeek knew the fix but couldn't EXPRESS it — the apply format couldn't represent the edit (a shape beyond replace/add/register).
categories.slot: a new op or a new registry in the allowlist
categories.recipe: WE add the op/registry under the same safety guards (dangerous-scan + ast re-parse), then expose it in build_prompt.
categories.risk: running model code — keep every op behind the existing sandbox + scan + re-parse gates.

categories.name: Path-B / human (disposition)
categories.symptom: Genuinely not auto-fixable from what we can give the model — the source is ambiguous, or the fix needs human structural judgement the gate can't verify.
categories.slot: na
categories.recipe: Log it for a human (GitHub issue / path-B). The quality guard correctly rejecting a sloppy fix is the right outcome here, not a failure.
categories.risk: the honest floor — don't force a confident-wrong fix to avoid this label.

categories.name: Fix pathway/source signal detection (harness)
categories.symptom: is_epub/is_pdf or pathway is derived from the converted output dir, which lacks the source (original.epub/ocr_response.json), so routing + the prompt's per-pathway caveats are wrong (e.g. a footnote_audit on an EPUB routes to process_document, not epub_normalizer; the EPUB sample/recipe hints never fire).
categories.slot: carry the detected pathway from the eval/case into load_artifacts instead of sniffing the book_dir, or also look at the case source dir
categories.recipe: WE thread the pathway/source signal through so modules_for() routes to the real responsible front-end and build_prompt() shows the right caveats + ★ categories.
categories.risk: low — diagnostic/routing only; but wrong routing wastes a whole expensive run.
